Hey, what's going on everyone? I wanted to make this little video just as an add-on so that you don't get overwhelmed. So if you look at this, this may be overwhelming to you. You're like, oh my god, there's so many things. But I just want you to note that I added a little bit of color to this so that yellows are things that you need to actually input. Not necessarily you, but need to be manually input. And anything that is white is something that is automatically going to calculate. I also did this nice sample column here so you can just get a feel for what these stats might look like. Some of these things are going to be monthly. So dropouts, things like that, these are things that you can be calculating on a monthly basis. And so some of this, the lifetime value here wouldn't really make sense because it makes it seem like you only had one person who exited per month, but it's really for that day. So that's why lifetime value, we don't really calculate that on a daily basis. It's definitely a monthly figure, if not longer. But I just want you to see how it all should look as you're putting this in. And just don't freak out. That's kind of the big point here is that a lot of times I hear people who are like, man, I feel like it's like this. I feel like we do better than that. I feel like we should. It's like your feelings don't matter. It's like you just, one of the biggest pieces of advice that I ever got was from a coach that I had and he was like, just look. And he just kept saying, every time I'd be like, well, what about, I kind of feel like I'd be like, just look. And so he's like, stop talking to me. He's like, just look. And so that was a huge lesson for me because it basically was like your feelings don't matter. And so when you have numbers, then you can make good decisions because you have them. And so as an added thing, what I did was I added the core function that each of these kind of belong to. So you can see whether you're having more legion issues, more lead nurture issues, sales, fulfillment, resell, that kind of thing. And I would say the big hitters are the ACQ or the biz numbers. And so they're the ones that combine multiple functions. You know what I mean? Like percentage of leads closed is a heavy hitter number. Like that matters a ton, right? Cost per acquisition matters a ton. Net starts combines, that's actually just sales. Sorry. But the acquisition here, like these numbers are heavy hitters, right? Because those are combined, not just sales, but legion, lead nurture and sales into one number for acquisition. What percentage of leads are we closing? What's our cost of acquisition? Those are the big hitter numbers. If you see, you know, biz, those are total business instruction operations types numbers. So like total EFTs is, sorry, net clients is influenced by all of these things, right? Whether you're gaining or losing clients is a function of how well you're selling. It's really how many leads you're getting, how many are coming, how many you're selling, how many you're keeping, like that's all the functions of the business. And so that like net clients number is going to be influenced by the entire business. Net revenue overhead, same thing, profit and margin. So I just, I had to add two things to this because from a stat standpoint, not everything falls neatly into one of the five four functions. But if you're having issues, you can see if the commonalities are more on the acquisition side or if your common, if your issues are more here. And if you're like, man, I can't find this data, then that's where it's, it comes down to consistently putting the process in place so that your operations manager, the end of the week, that's part of what he or she needs to turn into you is that these stats are updated and they're accounted for. The other piece that I'm just repeating this one more quick time is that not all these stats are done every day. A lot of these are not done every day. I just wanted to give you an example so you could at least see these things calculated out and I didn't want to mess with the column so that, you know, I didn't go all the way over because then you forget which one was monthly. So you know, like here it says lifetime value is 18,000. If that were the case, then we'd all be very, very wealthy. But that's just because it's one cancel for that day and you would never really count lifetime value on a daily basis. You'd be looking at it more monthly or quarterly because it's definitely a longer term stat that you need to track. And so that's why counting some of these stats on a daily basis don't really provide any value. So when you're looking at these stats, think is this valuable to me to know if it's not, do it either on a longer cadence so you don't need to check it every day or don't look at it at all. For me, these are all the things that I would want to know so that I can make decisions about the business. So I hope this helps, hope this makes sense and it's more important that you're consistent with it than like you go really hard for four days. All right, like consistency is everything here and data is only good as the amount of data you're pulling from. And I will say this, if the data is incomplete for a data set and you're missing a week and that's where you had a lot of your attrition, it can become a problem. So just be consistent about it. And then the nice thing is the more consistent you are with it, then the larger pool of data you have and the less likely that a single mess up or two will actually throw off your entire data set. So I just wanted to have this extra video in here because I felt like some people needed to hear it. Remember like there's fewer daily inputs than weekly and monthly and if you're like, man, I don't have time to do this. If you don't have time to understand what is going on in your business, then you shouldn't have one. Like there's no way you can improve something if you don't even know where you're at. So I'm just being clear here. Like it's like, oh man, this is extra work. Like yes, this is extra work. But the reason that you don't know what's wrong with your business is because you don't know. You feel like X. But until you know, it's irrelevant. So I hope this makes sense. Use this, swipe it, adjust it for your own needs and just be consistent. And you will read the rewards.
